title: Default-Lane Family Ablation
decision_question: Which signal families belong in the default agent lane when the lead list is capped to one to three primary actions?
hypothesis: Clone, obligation, boundary, and patch-local concentration signals will outperform policies that elevate broad structural pressure into the lead lane.

variants.name: Current policy
variants.description: Current ranking, watchpoint suppression, and tie-breaker behavior.

variants.name: Clone, obligation, rules
variants.description: Keep only clone, propagation, boundary, and direct obligation families eligible for the default lane.
variants.policy_delta: Remove structural pressure families from lead eligibility.

variants.name: Add patch-local concentration
variants.description: Allow patch-local concentration only when the patch itself worsened reviewability or branching pressure.
variants.policy_delta: Patch-local concentration can fill a lead slot if it is causal and repairable.

variants.name: No structural pressure in default lane
variants.description: Demote all structural pressure findings to supporting evidence or maintainer watchpoints.
variants.policy_delta: Suppress large_file and broad concentration findings from the lead lane.

variants.name: Patch-worsened structural pressure only
variants.description: Only allow structural pressure when the patch directly worsened it and the fix surface is concrete.
variants.policy_delta: Require patch-local causality and a clear fix surface for structural pressure to rank.

exit_bar: Screen enough repos to prove the default lane stays within one to three primary actions.
Shortlist two lead-lane policies for confirmation using measured help, follow, and patch-expansion tradeoffs.
Reject any policy that improves follow rate without improving help rate.
